title_en: Xbox Boss Asha Sharma Announces 3,200 Layoffs, Including 1,600 Today, With 4 Studios Leaving for New Management — Read the Email to Staff in Full
summary_en: Microsoft has announced 3,200 staff are set to lose their jobs at Xbox across the current financial year, with 1,600 of those layoffs happening today, July 6. As part of the restructure, four studios are leaving Xbox to new management, and one is now in consultation over its future.
body_en:
Microsoft has announced 3,200 staff are set to lose their jobs at Xbox across the current financial year, with 1,600 of those layoffs happening today, July 6. As part of the restructure, four studios are leaving Xbox to new management, and one is now in consultation over its future.
In an email sent to staff and reviewed by IGN, Xbox boss Asha Sharma called it the most “significant” restructure in Xbox history, insisting Microsoft’s gaming business “is not healthy.” Overall, Microsoft's gaming business is losing about one-fifth of its staff.
South of Midnight developer Compulsion Games and Psychonauts developer Double Fine Productions will return to management and transition to independent studios with their IP, catalog, and with “runway for their next games,” Sharma said. These games are so far unannounced. In Double Fine's case, it leaves Microsoft with the Psychonauts IP.
Meanwhile, Hellblade developer Ninja Theory and State of Decay developer Undead Labs “have entered terms to join new ownership with funding to complete and grow Senua and State of Decay 3.” This suggests both games will release eventually, developed under new ownership. Microsoft did not say who the new owners are.
Marvel’s Blade developer Arkane will now see its management enter the required consultation with its Works Council to “review potential strategic options,” per French labor law, with a view to potentially selling or spinning out the studio. There’s no word on the fate of Marvel’s Blade specifically.
Elsewhere, there are layoffs across other parts of Xbox, including Activision, Bethesda/ZeniMax, Blizzard, King, Mojang, and Xbox Game Studios. Microsoft insisted none of its first-party publicly announced games or projects are being cancelled as part of these reductions. That means the likes of Fable, Gears of War: E-Day, Clockwork Revolution, Call of Duty: Modern Warfare 4, Minecraft Dungeons 2, and The Elder Scrolls 6 are safe.
Bethesda also owns id Software, which makes Doom and Quake games. MachineGames makes Wolfenstein and, most recently, Indiana Jones. Activision Blizzard makes Call of Duty, World of Warcraft, Diablo, Overwatch, and Candy Crush.
These devastating cuts follow last July's round of layoffs at Microsoft, when 9,000 lost their jobs across the entire business. A number of games were canceled as part of those cuts, including Rare’s Everwild, and studios shut down, such as Perfect Dark developer The Initiative. Now, just 12 months later, we're here again, although this time Microsoft has managed to find a path forward for affected studios.
A recent report claimed Sharma wanted to speed up development on new The Elder Scrolls, Fallout, and Halo games as part of the company reset. Details on the potential shakeup came from The Information (via Reuters ), which said that Microsoft hadn't ruled out turning the Xbox brand into a wholly-owned subsidiary. It's said the move could result in Xbox being operated as a joint venture with other partners or even potentially sold.
We have much more on this developing story over at our live blog , which we will continue to update as today's news unfolds.
Here is the email in full as part of this developing story:
Wesley is Director, News at IGN. Find him on Twitter at @wyp100. You can reach Wesley at wesley_yinpoole@ign.com or confidentially at wyp100@proton.me.
